name.en-US: Refutation of Teaching that Deters from Religious Life (Contra Retrahentes)

description.en-US: A late polemical work defending the entry of young men into religious life against Gerard of Abbeville's attacks on mendicant recruitment.
description.la: Opus polemicum serotinum, ingressum iuvenum in vitam religiosam contra impugnationes Gerardi de Abbatisvilla in mendicantium receptione defendens.

sources.description: English translation by John Procter OP (Burns and Oates, London, 1902, PD), revised by the Aquinas Institute. Mirrored from aquinas.cc.
Latin text from the Leonine / Marietti editions (public domain), mirrored from aquinas.cc.

title.en-US: Chapter 1 — Object of the author in undertaking this work
Chapter 2 — Arguments used by those who maintain that none should be admitted to the religious life who are not practised in the exercise of the commandments
Chapter 3 — The foregoing arguments do not hold good in the case of children
Chapter 4 — The opinion held by Vigilantius and his followers does not apply to recent converts to the faith
Chapter 5 — This argument is equally fallacious as applied to penitent sinners
Chapter 6 — The fundamental error of these opinions exposed
Chapter 7 — The arguments of our opponents are conclusively refuted
Chapter 8 — Arguments used to prove that before entering religious life a man ought to deliberate for a long time, and take counsel of many
Chapter 9 — Answers to the foregoing arguments
Chapter 10 — Answer to the objections raised against the foregoing arguments
Chapter 11 — Arguments used by those who maintain that men should not bind themselves by vow to embrace the religious life
Chapter 12 — Refutation of the error contained in the last chapter
Chapter 13 — Refutation of the arguments adduced in the last chapter
Chapter 14 — Arguments against the perfection of religious whose possessions are not in common
Chapter 15 — Refutation of the errors quoted in the last chapter
Chapter 16 — An answer to the arguments which are brought forward against the propositions contained in the preceding chapter
